result.summary: Found one blocking issue in the diff. I could not run tests because `uv` and `pytest` are not installed in this runner.

findings.title: Authorization is collected but never enforced
findings.body: `/move` now accepts an `Authorization` header, but requests without or with invalid credentials still proceed. The header defaults to an empty string, `verify_auth_token()` currently always returns `True`, and the route ignores the return value anyway. That means this change creates the appearance of an auth boundary without rejecting any caller, so an unauthenticated `POST /move` keeps returning a move.
findings.recommendation: Make verification fail closed: require the header, validate it against the configured secret/provider, and raise `HTTPException(status_code=401 or 403)` when validation fails. Add tests for missing, invalid, and valid authorization headers.

next_steps: Install/use the project test environment and run the engine tests after fixing auth enforcement.
Add regression tests that prove unauthenticated `/move` requests are rejected.
